site.title: Bryon's OS
site.tagline: A living dashboard that turns chaos into motion.
site.description: A public operating board for Bryon's real work, real life, and real next moves — built to make priorities visible, decisions lighter, and momentum easier to keep.

data.title: Sick Sales Command Deck
primaryFocus: Build a daily operating system that moves more shirts and stickers
Make the hero SKU, offer, channel, and KPI visible in one place
Turn merch pushes into repeatable attacks instead of random swings

current.content:
This is the merch attack board.

The point is not to admire the system.

The point is to know what is being sold, why it should move today, where it is being pushed, and what number proves the push worked.

data.title: Sick Sales War Room
data.summary: Build a simple, ruthless operating system for selling more shirts and stickers by making the offer, channel, metrics, and next actions visible every day.
data.nextAction: Run the daily sales attack cadence around hero SKU, bundle push, email angle, social angle, homepage slot, and experiment tracking.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

Money is the job.

If shirts and stickers need to move, the system cannot stay fuzzy. The team needs one place that answers:

- what are we pushing right now
- what product should get the main slot
- what bundle should raise average order value
- what channel should carry the message today
- what numbers matter enough to watch daily
- what experiment is live and whether it worked

## Current attack model

- pick one hero product, not ten equal products
- pair it with one sticker or add-on bundle to lift AOV
- drive one clear angle across site, email, and social the same day
- update a tiny scorecard daily so momentum is visible
- rotate creative based on hooks, not random guessing

## Sales lanes

- Hero SKU: the one shirt or lead product getting the push
- Fast cash add-on: sticker pack, slap, or low-friction extra item
- Bundle move: shirt + sticker, event tee + decal, limited drop + free ship threshold
- Channel pressure: homepage, email, SMS if available, IG feed, IG story, reels, paid if active
- Conversion help: cleaner product naming, tighter first image, urgency, social proof, threshold offers

## Non-negotiables

- one main push at a time
- every campaign needs a hook
- every hook needs a KPI
- if a product is not moving, change the angle, image, placement, or offer quickly
- log experiments so the same bad idea does not get repeated as a new idea

## Morning questions

- What are we selling today?
- Why should someone care today?
- Where will they see it first?
- What add-on increases cart value?
- What number tells us by tonight if it worked?

data.title: SOABOS Dashboard
data.summary: Build a public home base that makes active work, real priorities, and the next move easier to see.
data.nextAction: Keep folding real operating systems into the board while making the design feel cleaner and easier to scan.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

Bryon needs one clean place to see what is in motion across life, work, and active builds without hunting through scattered apps and half-finished notes.

## Current direction

- content-first architecture
- public-safe by default
- conversation-driven updates
- publish fast without hand-rebuilding the site every time

data.title: Protect momentum across work and life
data.summary: Build a practical operating rhythm that supports work, family, and personal systems without making everything feel heavy.

goals.content: Momentum compounds when the next move is visible and the system is light enough to keep using.

data.title: Publish updates without cleanup headaches
data.summary: Make the dashboard reliable to ship so content changes can go live without broken paths, stray build artifacts, or manual patchwork.

goals.content: A system people trust gets updated. A system that feels fragile gets avoided.

data.title: Replace placeholders with real operating details
data.summary: Fill the dashboard with real projects, real focus, and useful descriptions so it starts feeling like a true working board.

goals.content: The dashboard should reflect reality early, even if it is still rough around the edges.

data.title: Reduce drift between ideas and execution
data.summary: Create enough structure that important work does not disappear between conversations, context switches, or busy days.

goals.content: The target is not maximum organization. It is keeping good work from slipping through the cracks.

data.title: Make priorities visible enough to act on
data.summary: Build a dashboard that keeps active projects, next actions, and useful progress visible without turning into clutter.

goals.content: The win is not more documentation. The win is less drift, less hunting, and faster motion.

data.title: Personal
data.summary: The routines and systems that help life feel steadier, more intentional, and less reactive.

areas.content: This area is where reflection, habits, and quality-of-life support systems live.

data.title: Systems
data.summary: The dashboards, workflows, and operating structure that keep chaos from swallowing the important stuff.

areas.content: This area holds the backbone work: dashboards, routing, process design, and the tools that make the rest of the machine easier to run.

data.title: Work
data.summary: Client work, creative experiments, and business-facing projects that need to ship, perform, or stay organized.

areas.content: This area covers the projects tied to Bryon's day-to-day output, revenue, and brand-building work.

updates.content:
Created the initial repo structure for a public dashboard that can track goals, projects, areas, and current focus.

Established a content-first model so future updates can be made by editing structured files rather than manually changing the website for every small change.

data.title: Added sharper operating language and next-move structure

updates.content: Started shaping the dashboard around a more usable rhythm: what matters now, what happens next, what is in the way, and what already moved forward.

data.title: Publish flow cleanup and first real content pass

updates.content: Started replacing scaffold copy with real operating details, added more actual projects and goals, and tightened the publish path so future updates can go out with less cleanup.

data.title: Expanded dashboard into a real operating board

updates.content: Added more actual operating areas, seeded real project tracks across work, events, home, and systems, and moved the dashboard closer to a usable public view instead of a generic scaffold.

data.title: Added birthday tracking lane and refreshed the dashboard feel

updates.content: Pulled the birthday system into the dashboard, refreshed the current focus around real motion, and tightened the presentation so the board feels cleaner, newer, and more ready for ongoing updates.

data.title: Reframed the dashboard as a live command deck

updates.content:
Shifted the dashboard away from generic project-tracker language and closer to Bryon's actual operating style: cleaner decisions, clearer motion, and one visual board that can steer life, work, and active builds without feeling bloated.

The focus now is not just listing projects. It is helping the next move stand out fast.

data.title: Built phase 2 into a stronger command center

updates.content:
Added a more decisive homepage structure with ranked priority stacking, clearer operating lanes, a tighter updates feed, and a stronger mobile-first scan path.

The board now behaves more like a live command center and less like a flat collection of cards.

data.title: Added phase 3 gratitude lane to the dashboard

updates.content:
Built a dedicated daily gratitude widget into the command deck so today's prompt can live on the site as well as in Telegram.

The board now shows the active prompt, Entry ID, pending/completed state, and a short recent-history trail so the gratitude system is easier to use at night without scrolling back through chat.

data.title: Added phase 4 event and workout lanes

updates.content:
Extended the command deck with two more real-use lanes: an event-prep view for DJ work and a workout lane for training momentum.

Both lanes now follow the same content-first pattern as the gratitude lane, so the homepage can show upcoming event pressure, next prep moves, workout consistency, and the next session without hardcoding the UI each time.

data.title: Added the scorecard, offer calendar, and experiment log

updates.content:
Built the operating files behind the sales push so the team can run a repeatable daily cadence instead of guessing.

That includes a scorecard template, an experiment log, an offer calendar, and a direct playbook for how to attack shirts and stickers.

data.title: Built the Sick sales war room into the dashboard

updates.content:
Added a dedicated Sick Sales War Room project plus a practical operating layer behind it: sales system notes, scorecard template, experiment log, offer calendar, and a concrete merch triage model.

The goal is simple: make shirts and stickers easier to push by turning the sales attack into a visible daily system instead of a vague intention.

The merch triage pass reframes the product sheet from a narrow promo-eligibility filter into an operator action board with four buckets:
- Push Now
- Bundle / Test
- Protect
- Dead / Review

A rough dry run against the current workspace export produced:
- Push Now: 32
- Bundle / Test: 258
- Protect: 83
- Dead / Review: 48
